title: Preparation of cigarette smoke extract (CSE)
steps: All procedures should be done in a fume hood to minimize exposure of the experimenter and laboratory environment to the cigarette smoke. The CSE should be prepared immediately before use.

1000 µl pipette tip Silicone tubing (Tigers Polymer, SR-1554, inner diameter: 3 mm, outer diameter: 5 mm, length: 250 mm) Polypropylene tubing (inner diameter: 2 mm, outer diameter: 4 mm, length: 150 mm) Polypropylene tubing (inner diameter: 5 mm, outer diameter: 7 mm, length: 75 mm) Silicone stopper with two holes 50 ml polypropylene conical tube (Thermo Fisher Scientific, 339657) Extension tubing (Terumo, SF-ET5527, 750 mm) Three-way stopcock (Terumo, TS-TR2K) 50 ml syringe (Terumo, SS-50LZ) phosphate-buffered saline (PBS) Millex-LG sterilizing filter unit (Merck/Millipore, SLLG025SS, 0.2 µm) Cigarettes
Fill the 50 ml polypropylene conical tube with 10 ml of PBS (6), and cap it with the silicone stopper (5).
Attach the 50 ml syringe (9) to the 3-way stopcock (8) connected to the extension tubing (7).
Insert the cigarette into the 1000 µl pipette tip (1), and attach it to the silicone tubing (2).
Light the cigarette while pulling on the syringe plunger.
Draw the syringe plunger to the 50 ml mark in 10 seconds (flow rate: 300 ml/min). The mainstream smoke is bubbled through PBS in the conical tube.
Turn the stopcock off to the extension tubing and push the plunger back into the syringe.
Open the stopcock to the extension tubing.
Repeat the steps 8-10 until the cigarette burns to the filter (typically 10 to 11 times per cigarette).
Remove the cigarette butt and insert the new cigarette to the 1000 µl pipette tip for the next round of extraction.
Repeat the procedure for a total of 5 cigarettes (smoke from 5 cigarettes is bubbled through 10 ml of PBS).
Filter the extract through the 0.2 µm pore size membrane, and the resulting solution is defined as 100% CSE.
creator: Yoshiyuki Matsuo
